title: Synthetic Biology Automation
coreConcept: The integration of robotics and microfluidics to automate the Design-Build-Test-Learn (DBTL) cycle in genetic engineering.
theory:
## The Automated Bio-Foundry

### Design Automation
Using Computer-Aided Design (CAD) tools to specify genetic circuits, which are then converted into robotic instructions for liquid handling robots.

### Automated 'Build'
Robotic workstations perform DNA assembly (e.g., Gibson Assembly) and transformation into host cells. This relies on the **Kinematics** and **Embedded Control** from lower tiers.

### Microfluidic 'Test'
High-throughput screening of engineered variants using **Microfluidic Architectures** to measure phenotypic responses at the single-cell level.
keyTakeaways: Close the DBTL loop with automated robotic workflows.
Program liquid-handling robots for genetic assembly.
Utilize microfluidics for high-speed phenotype screening.
Reduce human error in complex multi-step bio-protocols.
